nodes.label: GameScene Orchestration Shell Principle
nodes.rationale: GameScene.swift must stay an orchestration shell: route input, own scene-level wiring, and delegate feature work to dedicated systems/controllers. New gameplay/UI flows must have owning files under Ester/Game/. This prevents GameScene from becoming a god class and enforces separation of concerns with explicit dependency injection.

nodes.label: Knowledge Graph First Workflow
nodes.rationale: Before any broad code search, consult the project knowledge graph via /graphify query. Treat graphify-out/graph.json as the primary codebase map. Prefer graph traversal over raw file exploration. The rationale is to reduce blind code searching and accelerate onboarding to unfamiliar code areas.

nodes.label: Goal Mode Discipline
nodes.rationale: When working in goal mode, define a concrete finish condition before open-ended exploration. Keep work bounded and stop when condition is met. If blocked, state the blocker and stop instead of looping.

nodes.label: Freesound SFX Workflow
nodes.rationale: Use local Freesound helper at Tools/freesound.cjs for game SFX. Do not generate synthetic placeholder SFX. Prefer Creative Commons 0 license; use Attribution only when sidecar .freesound.json metadata preserves attribution. Store SFX in Ester/Audio/.

nodes.label: GameScene.swift (Orchestration Shell)
nodes.rationale: Must remain thin orchestration shell: route input, own scene-level wiring, delegate feature work. New feature flows must not be placed here. Each map is a separate GameScene instance.
